step_by_step_thinking:
\boxed{A}
**Clinical Reasoning:** Major trauma triggers systemic inflammation, leading to neutrophil mobilization and activation. Activated neutrophils migrate to the lungs, releasing cytotoxic substances that disrupt the pulmonary endothelium, contributing directly to the development of acute respiratory distress syndrome (ARDS), consistent with both the "two-hit" model described in transfusion-related acute lung injury and findings related to pulmonary contusions.
